Pilot Kickoff · Acme Life Sciences · 5 Sites

Validating the power-intelligence wedge across four R&D campuses.

A 90-day pilot to quantify rectifier-health visibility, baseline electrical waste, and identify the expansion criteria for fleet rollout.

Mark Chung Verdigris CEO · Pilot Sponsor
June 15, 2026 v1 · pilot-kickoff-abcam-life-sciences-20260615-v1
02Why we're here

Acme Life Sciences's R&D campuses run cleanrooms 24/7, but rectifier failures still surprise the team.

From the November 2025 site assessment with your facilities and reliability leads.

Three findings from the assessment carried into this pilot:

  • Rectifier failures arrive without warning. Three unplanned outages in the last 12 months, two of them weekend-only events that took 6+ hours to recover.
  • BMS dashboards don't see electrical-waveform structure. Power factor and harmonic distortion show up only after a fault, never as a leading indicator.
  • Energy spend is an opaque budget line. ~$2.4M/year across the four campuses; no visibility into which circuits drive the bill.
02 / 08 Customer-Confidential
03What we agreed to

Pilot scope, locked at signature.

From the Master Services Agreement, Schedule 1.

Domain Scope Customer-side owner
Sites Four R&D campuses (Cambridge UK, Burlingame CA, Cambridge MA, Waltham MA) Facilities Director
Hardware Verdigris meters on 24 critical rectifier circuits, 6 per site Site Engineering Lead
Data 8 kHz waveform stream → Verdigris Cloud · 30-day retention IT / Network Lead
Software Signals dashboard access for 8 named operators Reliability Lead
Window 90 days from hardware-online date Pilot Sponsor
03 / 08 Customer-Confidential
04Expansion criteria

Three measurable outcomes that earn the path to fleet deployment.

Same wording as the contract. If we hit all three, we move to fleet rollout.

  • Rectifier-health visibility. Signals identifies the precursor to at least one rectifier-class fault during the 90-day window, with at least 14 days of advance warning.
  • Baseline electrical waste. Quantify the 4-campus waste floor across power factor, harmonic distortion, and circuit imbalance, expressed in $/year against the current $2.4M baseline.
  • Operator adoption. At least 6 of 8 named operators access Signals weekly across the second half of the pilot (post-day-45), unprompted.
04 / 08 Customer-Confidential

07The anchor

If we get one thing right, this is it.

Anchor outcome
14+ days

of advance warning on at least one rectifier-class fault during the 90-day window, sufficient for Acme Life Sciences to schedule a planned maintenance window rather than absorb an unplanned outage.

Everything else in the pilot — baseline waste quantification, operator adoption, fleet-rollout math — is in service of validating that this anchor outcome is repeatable across Acme Life Sciences's R&D footprint.

08 / 08 Customer-Confidential

Pilot Kickoff · Apex Trading Systems · 3 Colocation Halls

Putting power-quality eyes on the racks that carry the trade tape.

A 90-day pilot to quantify rack-level power-quality margin, baseline thermal-derate risk on the trading-engine rows, and define the criteria for site-fleet expansion.

Mark Chung Verdigris CEO · Pilot Sponsor
May 12, 2026 v1 · pilot-kickoff-apex-trading-20260512-v1
02Why we're here

Trading-engine racks run at 90%+ of nameplate, but power-quality margin is currently invisible.

From the April 2026 introductory call with the Apex Data Center Strategy team.

Three findings carried forward from the call:

  • Density is the constraint, not floor space. The trading-engine rows are pushed close to PDU thermal envelope to keep latency-critical workloads on copper distance; any unexpected derate event takes revenue-bearing capacity offline.
  • BMS dashboards stop at the PDU. Per-rack waveform structure, transient events, and harmonic distortion are not visible to the existing monitoring stack. Faults surface as alarms, never as leading indicators.
  • The infra team owns uptime but doesn't own the diagnostic data. When a rack misbehaves, root-cause investigation depends on vendor logs that arrive days later, in formats that don't cross-correlate with what the trading floor saw.
02 / 08 Customer-Confidential
03What we agreed to

Pilot scope, locked at signature.

From the Master Services Agreement, Schedule 1.

Domain Scope Customer-side owner
Sites Three colocation halls (one each: East Coast, Central, West Coast) Director, Data Center Strategy
Hardware Verdigris meters on 18 trading-engine PDU branch circuits, 6 per hall Site Infrastructure Lead
Data 8 kHz waveform stream → Verdigris Cloud · 30-day retention Network & Security Lead
Software Signals dashboard access for 6 named operators across infra + trading-platform engineering Trading Platform Reliability Lead
Window 90 days from hardware-online date Pilot Sponsor
03 / 08 Customer-Confidential
04Expansion criteria

Three measurable outcomes that earn the path to multi-hall rollout.

Same wording as the contract. If we hit all three, we move to fleet rollout.

  • Power-quality margin visibility. Signals identifies the precursor to at least one PDU-class derate event during the 90-day window, with at least 7 days of advance warning.
  • Density-headroom quantification. Quantify the safe per-rack density headroom across the three halls, expressed as %-of-nameplate the racks can sustain without entering the harmonic regime that triggered the original concern.
  • Operator adoption. At least 4 of 6 named operators access Signals weekly across the second half of the pilot (post-day-45), unprompted, and reference its data in at least one root-cause investigation.
04 / 08 Customer-Confidential

08 / 08 Customer-Confidential

First meeting · Data center reliability · 25 minutes

Verdigris is the power-intelligence layer your data center doesn't have yet.

An introduction for operators who already trust their BMS and know it isn't enough.

Mark Chung Verdigris CEO
2026-05-10 customer-101 · v3
02The wedge

BMS sees that a rectifier failed. We see it failing.

8 kHz waveform telemetry on every monitored circuit, 24/7, for the life of the asset.

Most data center reliability tools sample at 1 Hz or below. Faults precipitate in milliseconds. The math doesn't add up; the precursor signal is there, but the resolution to see it isn't.

Verdigris meters sample at 8 kHz — fine enough to read harmonic structure, capacitor aging, and circuit-imbalance trends weeks before they become outages. We've validated this across 800+ rectifiers in production at four named customers.

02 / 06 Public
03Evidence

Fortune 50 telecom. $1.3M to $3M recovered in deferred CapEx.

Three-year fleet projection across 800+ rectifiers. Anonymized; full case study at verdigris.co/resources/case-studies.

Outcome

Verdigris identified rectifier-aging precursors across the fleet, allowing the customer to defer ~$1.3M-$3M of CapEx-style replacements over a three-year horizon by extending the useful life of equipment whose health was previously assumed-failing rather than measured.

"Verdigris turned what looked like a refresh budget into a maintenance budget. Our finance team had to rebuild the model." — VP Operations, Fortune 50 Telecom

03 / 06 Public
04Where we fit

Below the BMS. Above the multimeter.

Verdigris doesn't replace your existing reliability tooling. It adds the layer that's missing.

BMS

Knows the room is too hot. Knows the door is open. Sees electrical faults after they trip a breaker.

Verdigris

Reads waveform structure 24/7. Catches the precursor signal weeks before the breaker trip.

Field tools

Multimeters, IR cameras, thermal probes. Used reactively when something is wrong. Useful, but local in time and space.

04 / 06 Public
05Why now

AI workloads are breaking the assumptions power systems were sized against.

  • Load swings are faster and larger. A GPU training run can pull 600 kW one second and 120 kW the next; rectifiers sized against steady-state 1990s assumptions degrade in ways their nameplates can't predict.
  • Capacity is the constraint, not capital. Hyperscaler-class operators routinely strand 20-40% of nameplate capacity to derating margins, because the alternative — operating closer to the limit — requires visibility nobody has.
  • The reliability gap is asymmetric. A 30-second outage in an AI training cluster costs more than a 30-day window in a data warehouse. The risk profile changed; the tooling didn't.
05 / 06 Public

Next step

A 90-minute deep dive with your reliability and facilities leads.

Agenda: walk one of your existing rectifier-failure incidents through what Verdigris would have shown you. We bring the analysis; you bring the post-mortem and one of your engineers. If it lands, we draft a pilot scope on the spot. Proposed: week of 2026-05-19.

06 / 06 Public

Architectural advantages · Decision aid

Seven decisions that determine whether your power-intelligence layer compounds — or stalls.

For data-center operators evaluating the next decade of electrical observability and orchestration.

  1. Choose vendors with open APIs. Closed integrations create switching costs that exceed the software bill.
  2. Insist on raw waveform telemetry. 1 Hz averages discard the precursors that cause the failures you care about.
  3. Separate BMS from the diagnostic layer. A vendor doing both optimizes one at the expense of the other.
  4. Meter where load matters, not where access is convenient. Critical-circuit telemetry beats fleet averages.
  5. Cellular-redundant ingest. Network outages don't pause electrical events.
  6. Validate derate margins against measured load. Conservative derating strands 20-40% of existing capacity.
  7. Make diagnostics a contract, not a feature. Reliability obligations need their own SLA.
The argument

The next decade of reliability isn't about adding redundancy. It's about seeing the precursors today's monitoring stack flattens away — and orchestrating against them.

Verdigris is the layer between BMS and physical asset that captures the high-resolution signal. We don't replace your stack; we turn the rest of it into evidence.

See what 8 kHz telemetry shows that your current stack misses. Schedule a 30-minute architecture review
